23 24 ## News 25 - [2026/01] 🔥 SGLang Diffusion accelerates video and image generation ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2026-01-16-sglang-diffusion/)). 26 - [2025/12] SGLang provides day-0 support for latest open models ([MiMo-V2-Flash](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-12-16-mimo-v2-flash/), [Nemotron 3 Nano](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-12-15-run-nvidia-nemotron-3-nano/), [Mistral Large 3](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/pull/14213), [LLaDA 2.0 Diffusion LLM](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-12-19-diffusion-llm/), [MiniMax M2](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-11-04-miminmax-m2/)). 27 - [2025/10] 🔥 SGLang now runs natively on TPU with the SGLang-Jax backend ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-10-29-sglang-jax/)). 28 - [2025/09] Deploying DeepSeek on GB200 NVL72 with PD and Large Scale EP (Part II): 3.8x Prefill, 4.8x Decode Throughput ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-09-25-gb200-part-2/)). 29 - [2025/09] SGLang Day 0 Support for DeepSeek-V3.2 with Sparse Attention ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-09-29-deepseek-V32/)). 30 - [2025/08] SGLang x AMD SF Meetup on 8/22: Hands-on GPU workshop, tech talks by AMD/xAI/SGLang, and networking ([Roadmap](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials/blob/main/slides/amd_meetup_sglang_roadmap.pdf), [Large-scale EP](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials/blob/main/slides/amd_meetup_sglang_ep.pdf), [Highlights](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials/blob/main/slides/amd_meetup_highlights.pdf), [AITER/MoRI](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials/blob/main/slides/amd_meetup_aiter_mori.pdf), [Wave](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials/blob/main/slides/amd_meetup_wave.pdf)). 31 32
33 More 34 35 - [2025/11] SGLang Diffusion accelerates video and image generation ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-11-07-sglang-diffusion/)). 36 - [2025/10] PyTorch Conference 2025 SGLang Talk ([slide](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials/blob/main/slides/sglang_pytorch_2025.pdf)). 37 - [2025/10] SGLang x Nvidia SF Meetup on 10/2 ([recap](https://x.com/lmsysorg/status/1975339501934510231)). 38 - [2025/08] SGLang provides day-0 support for OpenAI gpt-oss model ([instructions](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/8833)) 39 - [2025/06] SGLang, the high-performance serving infrastructure powering trillions of tokens daily, has been awarded the third batch of the Open Source AI Grant by a16z ([a16z blog](https://a16z.com/advancing-open-source-ai-through-benchmarks-and-bold-experimentation/)). 40 - [2025/05] Deploying DeepSeek with PD Disaggregation and Large-scale Expert Parallelism on 96 H100 GPUs ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-05-05-large-scale-ep/)). 41 - [2025/06] Deploying DeepSeek on GB200 NVL72 with PD and Large Scale EP (Part I): 2.7x Higher Decoding Throughput ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-06-16-gb200-part-1/)). 42 - [2025/03] Supercharge DeepSeek-R1 Inference on AMD Instinct MI300X ([AMD blog](https://rocm.blogs.amd.com/artificial-intelligence/DeepSeekR1-Part2/README.html)) 43 - [2025/03] SGLang Joins PyTorch Ecosystem: Efficient LLM Serving Engine ([PyTorch blog](https://pytorch.org/blog/sglang-joins-pytorch/)) 44 - [2025/02] Unlock DeepSeek-R1 Inference Performance on AMD Instinct™ MI300X GPU ([AMD blog](https://rocm.blogs.amd.com/artificial-intelligence/DeepSeekR1_Perf/README.html)) 45 - [2025/01] SGLang provides day one support for DeepSeek V3/R1 models on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs with DeepSeek-specific optimizations. ([instructions](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/tree/main/benchmark/deepseek_v3), [AMD blog](https://www.amd.com/en/developer/resources/technical-articles/amd-instinct-gpus-power-deepseek-v3-revolutionizing-ai-development-with-sglang.html), [10+ other companies](https://x.com/lmsysorg/status/1887262321636221412)) 46 - [2024/12] v0.4 Release: Zero-Overhead Batch Scheduler, Cache-Aware Load Balancer, Faster Structured Outputs ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-12-04-sglang-v0-4/)). 47 - [2024/10] The First SGLang Online Meetup ([slides](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-learning-materials?tab=readme-ov-file#the-first-sglang-online-meetup)). 48 - [2024/09] v0.3 Release: 7x Faster DeepSeek MLA, 1.5x Faster torch.compile, Multi-Image/Video LLaVA-OneVision ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-09-04-sglang-v0-3/)). 49 - [2024/07] v0.2 Release: Faster Llama3 Serving with SGLang Runtime (vs. TensorRT-LLM, vLLM) ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-07-25-sglang-llama3/)). 50 - [2024/02] SGLang enables **3x faster JSON decoding** with compressed finite state machine ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-02-05-compressed-fsm/)). 51 - [2024/01] SGLang provides up to **5x faster inference** with RadixAttention ([blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-01-17-sglang/)). 52 - [2024/01] SGLang powers the serving of the official **LLaVA v1.6** release demo ([usage](https://github.com/haotian-liu/LLaVA?tab=readme-ov-file#demo)). 53 54
55 56 ## About 57 SGLang is a high-performance serving framework for large language models and multimodal models. 58 It is designed to deliver low-latency and high-throughput inference across a wide range of setups, from a single GPU to large distributed clusters. 59 Its core features include: 60 61 - **Fast Runtime**: Provides efficient serving with RadixAttention for prefix caching, a zero-overhead CPU scheduler, prefill-decode disaggregation, speculative decoding, continuous batching, paged attention, tensor/pipeline/expert/data parallelism, structured outputs, chunked prefill, quantization (FP4/FP8/INT4/AWQ/GPTQ), and multi-LoRA batching. 62 - **Broad Model Support**: Supports a wide range of language models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, GPT, Gemma, Mistral, etc.), embedding models (e5-mistral, gte, mcdse), reward models (Skywork), and diffusion models (WAN, Qwen-Image), with easy extensibility for adding new models. Compatible with most Hugging Face models and OpenAI APIs. 63 - **Extensive Hardware Support**: Runs on NVIDIA GPUs (GB200/B300/H100/A100/Spark), AMD GPUs (MI355/MI300), Intel Xeon CPUs, Google TPUs, Ascend NPUs, and more. 64 - **Active Community**: SGLang is open-source and supported by a vibrant community with widespread industry adoption, powering over 400,000 GPUs worldwide. 65 - **RL & Post-Training Backbone**: SGLang is a proven rollout backend across the world, with native RL integrations and adoption by well-known post-training frameworks such as [**AReaL**](https://github.com/inclusionAI/AReaL), [**Miles**](https://github.com/radixark/miles), [**slime**](https://github.com/THUDM/slime), [**Tunix**](https://github.com/google/tunix), [**verl**](https://github.com/volcengine/verl) and more. 66 67 ## Getting Started 68 - [Install SGLang](https://docs.sglang.io/get_started/install.html) 69 - [Quick Start](https://docs.sglang.io/basic_usage/send_request.html) 70 - [Backend Tutorial](https://docs.sglang.io/basic_usage/openai_api_completions.html) 71 - [Frontend Tutorial](https://docs.sglang.io/references/frontend/frontend_tutorial.html) 72 - [Contribution Guide](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html) 73 74 ## Benchmark and Performance 75 Learn more in the release blogs: [v0.2 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-07-25-sglang-llama3/), [v0.3 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-09-04-sglang-v0-3/), [v0.4 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-12-04-sglang-v0-4/), [Large-scale expert parallelism](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-05-05-large-scale-ep/), [GB200 rack-scale parallelism](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-09-25-gb200-part-2/). 76 77 ## Adoption and Sponsorship 78 SGLang has been deployed at large scale, generating trillions of tokens in production each day. It is trusted and adopted by a wide range of leading enterprises and institutions, including xAI, AMD, NVIDIA, Intel, LinkedIn, Cursor, Oracle Cloud, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, AWS, Atlas Cloud, Voltage Park, Nebius, DataCrunch, Novita, InnoMatrix, MIT, UCLA, the University of Washington, Stanford, UC Berkeley, Tsinghua University, Jam & Tea Studios, Baseten, and other major technology organizations across North America and Asia. 79 As an open-source LLM inference engine, SGLang has become the de facto industry standard, with deployments running on over 400,000 GPUs worldwide. 80 SGLang is currently hosted under the non-profit open-source organization [LMSYS](https://lmsys.org/about/). 81 82 83 84 ## Contact Us 85 For enterprises interested in adopting or deploying SGLang at scale, including technical consulting, sponsorship opportunities, or partnership inquiries, please contact us at sglang@lmsys.org 86 87 ## Acknowledgment 88 We learned the design and reused code from the following projects: [Guidance](https://github.com/guidance-ai/guidance), [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm), [LightLLM](https://github.com/ModelTC/lightllm), [FlashInfer](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer), [Outlines](https://github.com/outlines-dev/outlines), and [LMQL](https://github.com/eth-sri/lmql). 89

input.description: Find SGLang API and type system
input.prompt:
Search the SGLang codebase to understand the frontend API and type system used for output constraints. Specifically:

1. Search for "__init__.py" files in the sglang package to see what's exported
2. Look for files that define user-facing APIs like `sgl.gen()`, `sgl.select()`, etc.
3. Search for "SglSamplingParams" or similar parameter classes
4. Search for how "json_schema" is used as a parameter - grep for "json_schema" across the codebase
5. Search for "regex" as a parameter to generation functions
6. Look for any "backend" or "interpreter" files that process these constraints
7. Search for "Grammar" or "grammar" related files as constrained generation often uses grammars
8. Look for "decode" or "logits" processing related to constraints

Report all file paths and the key code patterns you find. Include actual code snippets of how existing constraints are defined and processed.

content.text:
Search the SGLang codebase to understand the frontend API and type system used for output constraints. Specifically:

1. Search for "__init__.py" files in the sglang package to see what's exported
2. Look for files that define user-facing APIs like `sgl.gen()`, `sgl.select()`, etc.
3. Search for "SglSamplingParams" or similar parameter classes
4. Search for how "json_schema" is used as a parameter - grep for "json_schema" across the codebase
5. Search for "regex" as a parameter to generation functions
6. Look for any "backend" or "interpreter" files that process these constraints
7. Search for "Grammar" or "grammar" related files as constrained generation often uses grammars
8. Look for "decode" or "logits" processing related to constraints

Report all file paths and the key code patterns you find. Include actual code snippets of how existing constraints are defined and processed.

49	def set_default_backend(backend: BaseBackend):
50	    global_config.default_backend = backend
51

53	def flush_cache(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
54	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
55	    if backend is None:
56	        return False
57	
58	    # If backend is Runtime
59	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
60	        backend = backend.endpoint
61	    return backend.flush_cache()
62	
63	
64	def get_server_info(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
65	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
66	    if backend is None:
67	        return None
68	
69	    # If backend is Runtime
70	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
71	        backend = backend.endpoint
72	    return backend.get_server_info()

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154

16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event

129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

40	    def __init__(

44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,

62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,

67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162

134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:

136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,
126	        top_p,
127	        top_k,
128	        min_p,
129	        frequency_penalty,
130	        presence_penalty,
131	        ignore_eos,
132	        return_logprob,
133	        logprob_start_len,
134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,
138	        json_schema,
139	    )

223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )

304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )

223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,

245	        progress_bar: bool = False,

247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,

287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state

93	def run_program_batch(
94	    program,
95	    backend,
96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))
113	
114	    if generator_style:
115	        return _run_program_batch_generator(
116	            program,
117	            backend,
118	            batch_arguments,
119	            default_sampling_para,
120	            num_threads,
121	            progress_bar,
122	        )
123	
124	    # Original code path when generator_style=False
125	    if num_threads == 1:
126	        rets = []
127	        if progress_bar:
128	            for arguments in tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments):
129	                rets.append(
130	                    run_program(
131	                        program,
132	                        backend,
133	                        (),
134	                        arguments,
135	                        default_sampling_para,
136	                        False,
137	                        True,
138	                    )
139	                )
140	        else:
141	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
142	                rets.append(
143	                    run_program(
144	                        program,
145	                        backend,
146	                        (),
147	                        arguments,
148	                        default_sampling_para,
149	                        False,
150	                        True,
151	                    )
152	                )
153	    else:
154	        if progress_bar:
155	            pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments))
156	
157	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
158	            futures = []
159	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
160	                futures.append(
161	                    executor.submit(
162	                        run_program,
163	                        program,
164	                        backend,
165	                        (),
166	                        arguments,
167	                        default_sampling_para,
168	                        False,
169	                        True,
170	                    )
171	                )
172	                if progress_bar:
173	                    futures[-1].add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
174	
175	            rets = [f.result() for f in futures]
176	        rets[-1].sync()
177	
178	        if progress_bar:
179	            pbar.close()
180	
181	    return rets
182	
183	
184	def _run_program_batch_generator(
185	    program,
186	    backend,
187	    batch_arguments,
188	    default_sampling_para,
189	    num_threads,
190	    progress_bar,
191	):
192	    """Helper function that yields results one by one using chunking to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor."""
193	    if num_threads == 1:
194	        iterator = tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments) if progress_bar else batch_arguments
195	        for arguments in iterator:
196	            yield run_program(
197	                program,
198	                backend,
199	                (),
200	                arguments,

40	class Sampler(nn.Module):
41	    def __init__(self):
42	        super().__init__()
43	        self.use_nan_detection = get_global_server_args().enable_nan_detection
44	        self.tp_sync_group = get_tp_group().device_group
45	        if is_dp_attention_enabled():
46	            self.tp_sync_group = get_attention_tp_group().device_group
47	
48	        self.rl_on_policy_target = get_global_server_args().rl_on_policy_target
49	        # In RL on-policy mode, deterministic inference is automatically enabled.
50	        self.enable_deterministic = (
51	            get_global_server_args().enable_deterministic_inference
52	        )
53	        # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
54	        self.use_log_softmax_logprob = self.rl_on_policy_target is not None
55	        self.use_ascend_backend = get_global_server_args().sampling_backend == "ascend"
56	
57	    def _preprocess_logits(
58	        self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo
59	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
60	        """Apply custom logit processors and handle NaN detection."""
61	        # Apply the custom logit processors if registered in the sampling info
62	        if sampling_info.has_custom_logit_processor:
63	            apply_custom_logit_processor(logits, sampling_info)
64	
65	        # Detect and handle NaN values in logits
66	        if self.use_nan_detection and torch.any(torch.isnan(logits)):
67	            logger.warning("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
68	            logits = torch.where(
69	                torch.isnan(logits), torch.full_like(logits, -1e5), logits
70	            )
71	            if crash_on_warnings():
72	                raise ValueError("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
73	
74	        return logits
75	
76	    def forward(
77	        self,
78	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,
79	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
80	        return_logprob: bool,
81	        top_logprobs_nums: List[int],
82	        token_ids_logprobs: List[List[int]],
83	        positions: torch.Tensor,
84	    ):
85	        """Run a sampler & compute logprobs and update logits_output accordingly.
86	
87	        Args:
88	            logits_output: The logits from the model forward
89	            sampling_info: Metadata for sampling
90	            return_logprob: If set, store the output logprob information to
91	                logits_output
92	            top_logprobs_nums: Number of top lobprobs per sequence in a batch
93	            token_ids_logprobs: Per-sequence list of specific token IDs to retrieve
94	                logprobs for. Each element is a list of token IDs (or None) for one
95	                sequence in the batch. This is used in speculative decoding.
96	            positions: The positions of the tokens in the sequence. Used for deterministic sampling
97	                to get the unique seed for each position.
98	        """
99	        logits = logits_output.next_token_logits
100	
101	        # Preprocess logits (custom processors and NaN handling)
102	        logits = self._preprocess_logits(logits, sampling_info)
103	
104	        if sampling_info.is_all_greedy:
105	            # Use torch.argmax if all requests use greedy sampling
106	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.argmax(logits, -1)
107	            if return_logprob:
108	                original_logprobs = logprobs = torch.nn.functional.log_softmax(
109	                    logits, dim=-1
110	                )
111	        else:
112	            simple_sampling_case = (
113	                not sampling_info.need_top_p_sampling
114	                and not sampling_info.need_top_k_sampling
115	                and not sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling
116	            )
117	
118	            # If requested, cache original logprobs before temperature scaling.
119	            if return_logprob and SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
120	                original_logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
121	
122	            # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
123	            logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel = None
124	            if self.rl_on_policy_target is not None:
125	                # TODO: use more inplace ops to save memory
126	                logits_div_temperature = (
127	                    logits.bfloat16().div(sampling_info.temperatures).bfloat16()
128	                )
129	                logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel = torch.log_softmax(
130	                    logits_div_temperature, dim=-1
131	                )
132	                del logits_div_temperature
133	
134	            if self.use_ascend_backend:
135	                # Ascend backend: sample from logits directly.
136	                batch_next_token_ids, logprobs = self._forward_ascend_backend(
137	                    logits, sampling_info, simple_sampling_case, return_logprob
138	                )
139	            elif (
140	                self.use_log_softmax_logprob
141	                and self.enable_deterministic
142	                and simple_sampling_case
143	            ):
144	                # RL on-policy path: sample from logprobs to match the trainer.
145	                batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_logprobs(
146	                    logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel,
147	                    sampling_info,
148	                    positions,
149	                )
150	                if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
151	                    logprobs = logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel
152	            else:
153	                # Standard path: do softmax and sample from probs.
154	                logits.div_(sampling_info.temperatures)
155	
156	                # In-place op to save memory
157	                logits[:] = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
158	                probs = logits
159	
160	                batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_probs(
161	                    probs, sampling_info, positions, simple_sampling_case
162	                )
163	                if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
164	                    logprobs = (
165	                        logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel
166	                        if logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel is not None
167	                        else torch.log(probs)
168	                    )
169	                del probs
170	
171	        # Attach logprobs to logits_output (in-place modification)
172	        if return_logprob:
173	            if SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
174	                logprobs = original_logprobs
175	            self._attach_logprobs_to_output(
176	                logits_output,
177	                logprobs,
178	                top_logprobs_nums,
179	                token_ids_logprobs,
180	                sampling_info,
181	                batch_next_token_ids,
182	            )
183	
184	        self._sync_token_ids_across_tp(batch_next_token_ids, sampling_info)
185	
186	        return batch_next_token_ids
187	
188	    def _sample_from_probs(
189	        self,
190	        probs: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
192	        positions: torch.Tensor,
193	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
194	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
195	        """Sample from probability distribution (after softmax).
196	
197	        Used for standard sampling with flashinfer/pytorch backends.
198	        Handles both simple (direct multinomial) and complex (top-k/top-p/min-p) cases.
199	        """
200	        if simple_sampling_case:
201	            batch_next_token_ids = sampling_from_probs_torch(
202	                probs,
203	                sampling_seed=sampling_info.sampling_seed,
204	                positions=positions,
205	            )
206	        else:
207	            backend = get_global_server_args().sampling_backend
208	            if backend == "flashinfer":
209	                assert (
210	                    sampling_info.sampling_seed is None
211	                ), "Sampling seed is not supported for flashinfer backend"
212	                if sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling:
213	                    probs = top_k_renorm_prob(probs, sampling_info.top_ks)
214	                    probs = top_p_renorm_prob(probs, sampling_info.top_ps)
215	                    batch_next_token_ids = min_p_sampling_from_probs(
216	                        probs, sampling_info.min_ps
217	                    )
218	                else:
219	                    batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_sampling_from_probs(
220	                        probs.contiguous(),
221	                        sampling_info.top_ks,
222	                        sampling_info.top_ps,
223	                        filter_apply_order="joint",
224	                        check_nan=self.use_nan_detection,
225	                    )
226	            elif backend == "pytorch":
227	                # A slower fallback implementation with torch native operations.
228	                batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_min_p_sampling_from_probs_torch(
229	                    probs,
230	                    sampling_info.top_ks,
231	                    sampling_info.top_ps,
232	                    sampling_info.min_ps,
233	                    sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling,
234	                    sampling_info.sampling_seed,
235	                    positions,
236	                )
237	            else:
238	                raise ValueError(f"Invalid sampling backend: {backend}")
239	        return batch_next_token_ids
240	
241	    def _sample_from_logprobs(
242	        self,
243	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
244	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
245	        positions: torch.Tensor,
246	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
247	        """Sample from log-probabilities using the Gumbel trick.
248	
249	        Used for deterministic sampling with simple cases (no top-k/top-p/min-p).
250	        Requires sampling_seed to be set in sampling_info.
251	        """
252	        assert (
253	            sampling_info.sampling_seed is not None
254	        ), "sampling_seed is required for sampling from logprobs"
255	        sampled_index = multinomial_with_seed(
256	            logprobs, sampling_info.sampling_seed, positions
257	        )
258	        return sampled_index.view(-1).to(torch.int32)
259	
260	    def _sample_from_logits(
261	        self,
262	        logits: torch.Tensor,
263	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
264	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
265	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
266	        """Sample from temperature-scaled logits without softmax.
267	
268	        Used for the Ascend NPU backend which handles softmax internally.
269	        """
270	        if simple_sampling_case:
271	            probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
272	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.multinomial(probs, num_samples=1).view(-1)
273	            return batch_next_token_ids.to(torch.int32)
274	        else:
275	            assert (
276	                self.use_ascend_backend
277	            ), "Only ascend backend supports sampling from logits"
278	            batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_min_p_sampling_from_logits_ascend(
279	                logits,
280	                sampling_info.top_ks,
281	                sampling_info.top_ps,
282	                sampling_info.min_ps,
283	                sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling,
284	            )
285	            return batch_next_token_ids.to(torch.int32)
286	
287	    def _forward_ascend_backend(
288	        self,
289	        logits: torch.Tensor,
290	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
291	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
292	        return_logprob: bool,
293	    ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
294	        """Handle the full Ascend backend sampling path.
295	
296	        Ascend backend has fused kernels that handle softmax internally,
297	        so we sample directly from temperature-scaled logits.
298	
299	        Returns:
300	            A tuple of (batch_next_token_ids, logprobs). logprobs is None

39	                                "type": "string",
40	                                "description": "Location to get weather for",
41	                            },
42	                            "unit": {
43	                                "type": "string",

46	                            },

55	                    name="search",

58	                        "type": "object",

71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):
72	        """Test schema generation for tool_choice='required'"""
73	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, "required")

76	        jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
77	
78	        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")
79	        self.assertEqual(schema["minItems"], 1)
80	        self.assertIn("items", schema)
81	        self.assertIn("anyOf", schema["items"])
82	
83	        # Should have schemas for both tools
84	        self.assertEqual(len(schema["items"]["anyOf"]), 2)
85	
86	        # Check that each tool schema is present
87	        tool_names = [
88	            item["properties"]["name"]["enum"][0] for item in schema["items"]["anyOf"]
89	        ]
90	        self.assertIn("get_weather", tool_names)
91	        self.assertIn("search", tool_names)
